title: Sr. Director, Pharmacy Network Informatics
raw_jd:
Navitus Health Solutions

Sr. Director, Pharmacy Network Informatics

Navitus - Putting People First in Pharmacy - Navitus was founded as an alternative to traditional pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) models. We are committed to removing cost from the drug supply chain to make medications more affordable for the people who need them. At Navitus, our team members work in an environment that celebrates diversity, fosters creativity and encourages growth. We welcome new ideas and share a passion for excellent service to our customers and each other._____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________. Current associates must use SSO login option at https://employees-navitus.icims.com/ to be considered for internal opportunities. ____________________________________________________We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all applicants and employees and comply with all applicable nondiscrimination regulations, including those related to protected veterans and individuals with disabilities. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, or handicap.

 Pay Range 

USD $147,648.00 - USD $184,559.00 /Yr.

 STAR Bonus % (At Risk Maximum) 

17.50 - Sr Director

Work Schedule Description (e.g. M-F 8am To 5pm)

M-F 8am to 5pm CT

 Remote Work Notification 

ATTENTION: Navitus is unable to offer remote work to residents of Alaska, Hawaii, Maine, Mississippi, New Hampshire, New Mexico, North Dakota, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, West Virginia, and Wyoming.

Overview

Due to growth, we are adding a Sr. Director, Pharmacy Network Informatics to our team!

The Sr. Director, Pharmacy Network Informatics will oversee, lead and provide strategic direction to the Navitus Network Pricing Team, Pharmacy Business Impact Analysts, Retail and Specialty Contract Analysts, Network Reporting Team, Business Impact Analyst, Reconciliation, Pharmacy Contract Performance, and the Navitus MAC Team to ensure the success of business needs, financial guarantee, and risk analysis. The Sr. Director will ensure collaboration, partnerships and support of the Navitus Provider Services, Sales, Account Management, Underwriting, Finance, and Government Programs departments The Sr. Director will leverage use of emerging technologies and analytics to assist internal and external customers to make informed decisions to decrease Navitus’ financial risk and increase Navitus’ sales, technical advancements, and marketing opportunities and will serve as the Provider Services primary subject matter expert on all existing and prospective clients and RFP response.

Is this you? Find out more below!

Responsibilities

 How do I make an impact on my team ? 

     Direct the adequacy and performance of Navitus’ provider networks via data modeling, analysis, and reporting received from the team. 
     Develop and oversee pharmacy business intelligences dashboards, reports, and analytics infrastructure. 
     Partner with internal stakeholders such as Finance, Underwriting, Operations, Compliance, IT to align pharmacy performance metrics, forecasting , KPI’s with organizational goals. 
     Lead and effectively manage all risk based pharmacy performance projects to ensure proper reimbursement, risk mitigation management and forecasting to maintain contract compliance effectively communicating to internal and external stakeholders. Leverage large and complex datasets to model financial, clinical, and operational outcomes; translate findings into implementable strategies that optimize benefit design and network performance. 
     Effectively interpret data models to establish prospect and renewal pricing and forecast solutions to secure new/ retained business. 
     Support and advise senior leadership regarding provider agreements, administration of the MAC Program, and network pricing strategy for prospects and renewals. 
     Collaborate and lead informatics key objectives with Provider Services team leads in areas including retail and specialty contracting, pharmacy operations, and client pharmacy services support. 
     Responsible for maintaining and managing a competitive, market leading MAC Program including working with pharmacies, clients, vendors, and internal department liaisons to implement and maintain a compliant MAC Program . 
     Main oversight for monitoring and maintaining Navitus generic effective rate, cost-plus, and other contractual requirements and pharmacy pricing methodologies with applicable providers and facilitating adjustments in Navitus’ adjudication system to meet contractual terms. 
     Ownership of applicable data sources and models, including limitations of use and processes to ensure and enhance integrity. Applicable data sources and models include but are not limited to proprietary internal databases, PC2, Quest Analytics, Qlikview extracts, SQL, PAC, Medi-Span, HHSC, and CMS data files. 
     Defend governmental and commercial reports as requested in formal audits with third party auditors . Identify meaningful report validation techniques and participate in full validation of audited reports. 
     Ownership of the development and maintenance of Policy and Procedures as required by Navitus for all reports requiring formal P&Ps. 
     Oversee and identify opportunities for maximizing user self-service of pharmacy networks data . Work with the Manager, Pharmacy Network Reporting to identify other areas for improvement . Build solutions using the best-fit technology to push information gathering out to the end user 
     Lead and inspire high-performing teams by providing strategic direction, aligning talent and resources with organizational goals to drive operational excellence, foster cross-functional collaboration, and promote a culture of continuous improvement across key business initiatives. 
     Other duties as assigned 

Qualifications

 What our team expects from you? 

     Bachelor’s degree in information systems, statistics, finance, actuarial sciences, or business or equivalent work experience required . Additional work experience and/or courses in a health-related field, especially pharmacy benefit management, preferred. 
     10 + years’ experience in healthcare economics or PBM analytics required . 
     10+ years’ experience leading and developing multi-disciplinary teams required . Experience leading teams in PBM or managed care preferred. 
     Demonstrated experience building and operationalizing Pharmacy Network Informatics structures, frameworks, and modeling tools required . 
     Strong track record of leading processes and standard collaboration with external client facing teams. 
     5+ years’ experience leading informatics and developing data driven analysis to drive pricing methodologies. 
     Demonstrated ability to design long-term strategies to support business risk mitigation processes. 
     Participate in, adhere to, and support compliance program objectives . 
     The ability to consistently interact cooperatively and respectfully with other employees . 

 What can you expect from Navitus ? 

     Top of the industry benefits for H ealth, D ental, and V ision insurance 
     20 days p aid t ime o ff 
     4 weeks paid parental leave 
     9 paid holidays 
     401K company match of up to 5% - No vesting requirement 
     Adoption Assistance Program 
     Flexible Spending Account 
     Educational Assistance Plan and Professional Membership assistance 
     Referral Bonus Program – up to $750! 

 Location : Address 

Remote

 Location : Country 

US

Benefits found in job post

401(k)

score.skills_rationale: The role requires analytics, reporting, dashboard development, and data modeling - areas where the candidate has extensive experience. However, the pharmacy/PBM domain is highly specialized and the candidate lacks healthcare/pharmacy benefit management background. SQL and business intelligence tools align well.
score.scope_rationale: This Sr. Director role leading multiple teams and providing strategic direction matches the candidate's executive leadership experience perfectly. The scope of managing cross-functional teams, financial risk analysis, and serving as a subject matter expert aligns with the candidate's senior architect and leadership background.
score.pay_rationale: The stated range of $147K-$184K is below the candidate's W2 target of $160K-$220K, with the lower end falling short of expectations.
red_flags: Highly specialized PBM domain with 10+ years required
score.raw_analysis: This is a legitimate senior director role at a PBM company with clear responsibilities and competitive scope matching the candidate's leadership level. However, it requires deep pharmacy/healthcare domain expertise that the candidate lacks. The analytics and BI skills transfer well, but the specialized PBM knowledge gap is significant. Pay range is on the lower end of acceptable but role quality and seniority make it worth pursuing.

SUMMARY

Senior data and analytics leader with 25+ years of experience delivering complex informatics solutions, financial risk analysis, and strategic data-driven insights across large-scale enterprise systems. Currently leading data architecture and reporting for the Department of Veterans Affairs' Financial Management Business Transformation (FMBT) program, with deep expertise in building analytics infrastructure, managing cross-functional teams, and translating complex datasets into actionable business strategies.

Proven track record directing multi-disciplinary teams, developing pricing methodologies, and establishing data governance frameworks that drive operational excellence. Experienced in large dataset analysis, financial forecasting, contract performance monitoring, and building self-service analytics platforms. Known for bridging technical capabilities with business strategy to optimize performance, mitigate risk, and support organizational growth objectives.
